_meta.dna: global(reach) = locale(correct) x culture(respected) x script(rendered) x format(native)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7-10 seed questions. During execution, expand to 100 questions per persona by probing deeper into the domain.

name: Tower of the World
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_domain: English (en), 1.5B speakers, baseline native language
questions: Is the product fully functional in English with no untranslated strings or placeholders?
Does the product use en-US date formatting (MM/DD/YYYY) hardcoded or locale-aware formatting?
Are en-GB users served DD/MM/YYYY dates or incorrectly shown MM/DD/YYYY?
Is the currency symbol always $ or does it adapt based on the user's region?
Does toLocaleString('en-US') appear hardcoded in dashboard.js, credits-panel.js, or formatting.ts?
Can an en-GB user distinguish March 4 from April 3 in the date display?
Are all error messages, help text, and recipe descriptions complete and grammatically correct in English?

floors.persona_domain: Spanish (es), 559M speakers, Latin script
questions: Does the Spanish locale extend beyond the homepage into recipe descriptions and execution output?
Are there mixed Spanish-English strings in the approval dialog (e.g., 'Ejecutar OAuth3 recipe')?
Does the Spanish translation maintain consistent formal register (usted vs tu) throughout?
Are Spanish gendered nouns correctly applied to all product terms?
Is the evidence chain output available in Spanish or only English?
Does the app store display recipe descriptions in Spanish?
Are error messages translated into Spanish with correct grammar?

floors.persona_domain: French (fr), 321M speakers, Latin script with gender agreement
questions: Does the French locale use correct gender agreement for all nouns (la recette, not le recipe)?
Are there strings where the LLM treated English nouns as masculine when they should be feminine?
Is the formal/informal register consistent throughout the French locale?
Does a native French speaker detect machine translation within the first 30 seconds?
Are French accents (e, e, a, u, c) rendered correctly across all UI components?
Is the French locale complete beyond the homepage into recipe execution and evidence output?
Does the product handle French quotation marks (guillemets) correctly?

floors.persona_domain: Portuguese (pt), 264M speakers, pt-BR vs pt-PT distinction
questions: Does the Portuguese locale serve Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) or European Portuguese (pt-PT)?
Is there a separate pt-PT locale for European Portuguese speakers?
Does the translation use 'voce' (Brazilian) or 'tu' (European) consistently?
Are Brazilian Portuguese expressions that are incorrect in European Portuguese present?
Can a Lisbon user select European Portuguese or are they forced to read Brazilian?
Does the Intl API use pt-BR locale codes for formatting numbers and dates?
Are there dialect-specific terms that would confuse a speaker of the other variant?

floors.persona_domain: German (de), 134M speakers, formal/informal register
questions: Does the German locale maintain consistent formal address (Sie) or mix Sie and du?
Are German compound nouns handled correctly without breaking UI element widths?
Do umlauts (a, o, u) render correctly across all components?
Is the German locale structurally correct in grammar even if register varies?
Does mixing Sie and du in the same interface occur (equivalent to alternating Sir/Dude)?
Are German strings approximately 30-40% longer than English without overflowing containers?
Is overflow-wrap: break-word applied for German compound words?

floors.persona_domain: Italian (it), 68M speakers, verb conjugation
questions: Does the Italian locale contain verb conjugation errors (e.g., 'il sistema hanno' instead of 'il sistema ha')?
Are there strings where plural and singular agreement is wrong?
Can a native Italian speaker detect grammar errors within 30 seconds of browsing?
Is the Italian locale complete beyond the homepage into the full product?
Does the LLM-generated Italian maintain consistent register throughout?
Are Italian-specific characters (a, e, i, o, u) rendered correctly everywhere?
Does a published author (Italian) find the translation quality acceptable?

floors.persona_domain: Indonesian (id), 275M speakers, simple grammar
questions: Is the Indonesian locale clean (no gendered nouns, no conjugation, no plural markers to fail)?
Are currency displays showing Rp (Indonesian Rupiah) instead of $?
Are dates displayed in DD/MM/YYYY format instead of MM/DD/YYYY?
Is the Indonesian locale the cleanest non-English locale due to grammar simplicity?
Does the formatting still use en-US conventions despite having Indonesian strings?
Was the Indonesian locale tested by an Indonesian speaker?
Does the locale correctly handle Indonesian number formatting (period as thousands separator)?

floors.persona_domain: Vietnamese (vi), 85M speakers, Latin script with diacritics
questions: Do Vietnamese diacritics (6 tone marks x 12 vowels = 72 combinations) render correctly?
Are there any Thai script characters contaminating Vietnamese strings (vi.json line 223)?
Does the Vietnamese locale maintain consistent register (formal vs casual)?
Is Vietnamese the second-cleanest non-English locale after Indonesian?
Are date and currency formats Vietnamese-native (VND, DD/MM/YYYY)?
Does the text rendering engine handle Vietnamese combining marks correctly?
Are all 415 Vietnamese strings free of cross-script contamination?

floors.persona_domain: Russian (ru), 258M speakers, Cyrillic script
questions: Is the Russian locale free of cross-script contamination (no Latin, Chinese, Arabic)?
Does the Russian locale use natural grammar rather than machine-translation-sounding structures?
Are dates displayed in DD.MM.YYYY format (Russian standard)?
Is the ruble symbol used instead of $ for currency displays?
Are there case declension errors (e.g., 'dlya retsept' instead of 'dlya retsepta')?
Does Cyrillic render correctly across all three projects (browser, CLI, AGI)?
Is Russian the best non-English locale in the entire system (zero contamination)?

floors.persona_domain: Korean (ko), 82M speakers, Hangul script, 15.7% contamination
questions: What percentage of Korean strings contain characters from other scripts (target: 0%, actual: 15.7%)?
Are Chinese characters (Traditional, Simplified) present inside Korean locale strings?
Are Cyrillic, Vietnamese, or Arabic characters contaminating Korean strings?
Is the cross-script contamination from the Llama 3.3 batch translation pipeline?
Does a native Korean speaker detect contamination within the first 30 seconds?
Can a monolingual Korean user use the product without encountering foreign characters?
Does the Unicode range proximity (Korean U+AC00-D7AF, Chinese U+4E00-9FFF) explain the contamination?

floors.persona_domain: Hindi (hi), 609M speakers, Devanagari script
questions: Does Devanagari script render correctly across solaceagi.com?
Is Hindi locale contamination minimal (target: 0 strings, actual: 2 strings)?
Is the Hindi grammar natural rather than machine-translated-sounding?
Is the rupee symbol used instead of $ for currency?
Are dates displayed in DD/MM/YYYY format?
Does the Devanagari Unicode range (U+0900-097F) distinctness explain less contamination?
Can a monolingual Hindi speaker use the product without encountering non-Devanagari characters?

floors.persona_domain: Japanese (ja), 125M speakers, politeness registers
questions: Does the Japanese locale use polite forms (desu/masu) consistently in formal contexts?
Are there strings using casual forms (da/dearu) where polite forms are required?
Is the politeness level violation a social contract breach for Japanese users?
Are Japanese web fonts declared or does the product rely on system fonts only?
Do Hiragana, Katakana, and Kanji all render correctly?
Does the product handle Japanese text input (IME) correctly?
Is the Japanese locale grammatically correct even if the politeness register is wrong?

floors.persona_domain: Arabic (ar), 422M speakers, RTL script, triple failure
questions: Are 30+ Arabic strings contaminated with Chinese, Russian, Thai, Korean, or code fragments?
Is dir='rtl' set on the HTML element when Arabic locale is active?
Are the 170+ RTL CSS rules in solace-browser activated by any JavaScript code?
Does Arabic text display left-to-right (backwards) due to missing RTL activation?
Are Arabic font stacks declared in the CSS?
Can a monolingual Arabic speaker read any page in the product without encountering foreign script?
Is the RTL CSS dead code a promise the product wrote to itself and never kept?

floors.persona_domain: Turkish (tr), 88M speakers, dotless-i case mapping
questions: Do Turkish locale files exist across any of the three projects?
Does the product's string comparison code use locale-aware case folding for Turkish?
Would toLowerCase() and toUpperCase() produce wrong results for Turkish dotless-i?
Is Turkish the canary for locale-aware string handling in JavaScript?
If Turkish were added tomorrow, would case-insensitive comparisons work correctly?
Does the i18n infrastructure support Turkish-specific case mapping rules?
Are there tests for locale-sensitive string operations that would catch Turkish issues?

floors.persona_domain: Polish (pl), 45M speakers, complex plural rules
questions: Do Polish locale files exist across any of the three projects?
Does the product have a plural engine (ICU MessageFormat, i18next) for complex plurals?
Can Python str.format() or JS template literals handle Polish plural rules (1, 2-4, 5-21, 22+)?
Would 'You have {count} recipe' produce 'You have 5 recipe' without plural rules?
Are Polish 5 grammatical cases and 3 genders handled by any i18n infrastructure?
Is Polish the stress test for i18n infrastructure that the product fails?
Does any plural-aware formatting library exist in the project dependencies?

floors.persona_domain: Dutch (nl), 30M speakers, compound words
questions: Do Dutch locale files exist across any of the three projects?
Is overflow-wrap: break-word applied for Dutch compound words that exceed English lengths by 40%?
Would adding Dutch overflow every button and label sized for English string lengths?
Does the CSS handle long compound words with word-break or overflow strategies?
Is the i18n infrastructure mechanically capable of adding Dutch?
Are UI elements fixed-width or flexible enough to accommodate longer strings?
Does the product test with strings 40% longer than English to simulate German/Dutch?

floors.persona_domain: Thai (th), 71M speakers, scriptio continua (no word boundaries)
questions: Do Thai locale files exist across any of the three projects?
Does the product's text rendering handle scriptio continua (no spaces between words)?
Is there a word segmentation engine for Thai text line breaking?
Would Thai text render as one unbreakable line per paragraph?
Are Thai web fonts declared in the CSS?
Can the product display, segment, and wrap Thai text correctly?
Is the rendering infrastructure absent for Thai (not just locale files)?

floors.persona_domain: Bengali (bn), 272M speakers, 7th most spoken, Brahmic script

Does the product declare fonts for Bengali (Bangla) Brahmic script?

Is the absence of Bengali support a Western European + East Asian bias indicator?
Would system fonts on a non-Bengali device render Bengali text as tofu/squares?

floors.persona_domain: Swahili (sw), 200M speakers, Latin script, simple grammar
questions: Do Swahili locale files exist across any of the three projects?
Is Swahili the lowest-effort locale to add (Latin script, simple grammar, no gender, no complex plurals)?

Is the absence of Swahili (200M speakers, Latin script) a deliberate exclusion or oversight?
Does the product's locale list reflect a blind spot for African languages?
Can the i18n 4-step process add Swahili with string files alone?

Does the Russian locale infrastructure prove Cyrillic rendering works?

floors.persona_domain: boss floor: AI self-inspection -- can 95% of the world use Solace natively?

floors.persona_domain: boss floor: professional translator and diplomat -- does this product understand what translation IS?
questions: Can a monolingual speaker of each target language use Solace without encountering foreign characters?

In 2030 with 100 countries, does the current i18n infrastructure scale?
